articles.title: What will be the toll cost on Ganga Expressway between Meerut and Prayagraj?

articles.title: CRPF Constable recruitment 2026: Registration underway for 9,195 posts; check selection process, direct link to apply
articles.description: The Central Reserve Police Force has opened online applications for 9,195 Constable (Technical and Tradesmen and Pioneer) positions. The registration window is open from April 20 to May 19, 2026. Candidates need to be at least Class 10 pass. The selection involves physical tests, a computer-based exam, and a skill test.

articles.title: Lodha Developers aims 2.5-fold jump in annual net profit to Rs 8,500 cr by FY31: MD Abhishek Lodha
articles.description: Lodha Developers plans a significant profit increase by fiscal 2031. The company targets over Rs 8,500 crore in net profit. This growth is fueled by strong demand for homes and commercial properties. Lodha Developers has a robust development pipeline and a strong market position. The Indian housing market is expected to expand for decades.

articles.title: BMW M440i convertible launched in India at Rs...; Check features, performance, and engine
articles.description: BMW M440i convertible: The new model sits between everyday luxury convertibles and high-end sports cars. It is priced at Rs 1.09 crore (ex-showroom).

articles.title: Karnataka bus strike this month: Will city and intercity services be disrupted?
articles.description: Karnataka transport unions have announced an indefinite statewide bus strike from May 20, demanding a 25% salary hike and pending arrears. KSRTC, BMTC and other services may face major disruption.

articles.title: Fuel distribution stable, global situation affects gas prices: Joshi
articles.description: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi attributes rising gas prices to global factors and state taxes. Opposition leaders Vaiko and Rahul Gandhi strongly condemn the sharp hike in commercial LPG cylinder prices, warning of widespread economic impact.

articles.title: Kudankulam project: AERB permits equipment erection for Units 5, 6
articles.description: The Atomic Energy Regulatory Board (AERB) has greenlit the erection of major equipment for Units 5 and 6 at the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Project, marking a significant milestone for India's nuclear energy programme and its clean power goals.

articles.title: Tripura CM Manik Saha calls for timely completion of urban projects
articles.description: Tripura CM Manik Saha reviewed urban development projects, directing officials to expedite work on projects like the Lighthouse Project and city hospital. He stressed timely completion, quality, and regular monitoring for better citizen services.

articles.title: Nearly a decade after demonetisation seized his Rs 2 lakh, how a Pune contractor finally won it back
articles.description: "It should not take these many years to get what is legally ours," says Pune contractor Girish Malani, who spent nearly a decade fighting the RBI in court after police seized his demonetised notes at an election check-post in 2016.

articles.title: 30-year-old judge found dead at Delhi home
articles.description: On Saturday, a somber shadow fell over South Delhi as 30-year-old Aman Sharma, a dedicated judge and secretary of the District Legal Services Authority, took his own life at his home. Family alarms raised, prompting police intervention; emergency teams had to force entry into his locked bathroom to determine his condition.

The company, which manufactures power and distribution transformers, has benefited from strong investor interest in India’s power transmission and capex cycle.

articles.title: CEA Nageswaran calls for capex push amid EV growth momentum
articles.description: India's top companies saw profits soar post-Covid. However, investments lagged. Chief economic adviser V Anantha Nageswaran called for corporate introspection. He highlighted that companies accumulated cash instead of investing in real assets. This forced the public sector to drive growth. Nageswaran stressed the need for private sector investment to seize domestic opportunities and address trade deficits.

articles.title: PSPCL moves HC against deadline to pay DA to employees, pensioners

This comes as Oracle also plans substantial job cuts, highlighting financial pressures despite efforts to secure funding.

articles.title: Om Birla rejigs 4 House panels for 2026-27; most chiefs stay on
articles.description: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla has reconstituted four key financial and department-related parliamentary committees. These committees will serve for the 2026-27 financial year. Several prominent leaders have retained their chairpersonships. Faggan Singh Kulaste heads the Committee on the Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes. Baijayant Jay Panda chairs the Committee on Public Undertakings.

articles.title: 19 years on, demand for direct train from Vizag to Bengaluru remains unfulfilled
articles.description: Despite rising demand, a direct train from Vizag to Bengaluru remains unfulfilled, hindering economic growth between the two IT hubs.

articles.title: Chief Minister Lalduhoma attends Mizoram Rural Bank Branch Managers’ Conclave 2026
articles.description: Mizoram Rural Bank is recognized as one of India's most progressive Regional Rural Banks. The bank achieved a remarkable Credit-Deposit Ratio of 63.28 percent. Its total business turnover reached over 10,600 crore rupees. Mizoram Rural Bank also leads in digital transactions in Northeast India. The Chief Minister lauded its contribution to state development.

articles.title: Plurilaterals without guardrails can fragment trading system: Experts
articles.description: Global trade volumes are growing despite concerns about the WTO. Experts discussed reforming the WTO's rules and governance. They highlighted the rise of 'precautionism' and the need for balanced reform. India's role in plurilateral negotiations was debated. The WTO remains indispensable for global trade and multilateralism.

articles.title: J&K PMGSY Funds: Udhampur-Kathua-Doda gets massive 43% share
articles.description: Jitendra Singh's Udhampur-Kathua-Doda constituency secures a staggering ₹1,524.46 crore, nearly 43% of the total ₹3,566 crore PMGSY-IV allocation for Jammu and Kashmir, aimed at boosting rural infrastructure and last-mile connectivity.

articles.title: Palamu District Gears Up for House Listing and Census: May 16 - June 14
articles.description: Palamu district is set to conduct its house listing and census from May 16 to June 14, involving 3,816 enumerators and 722 supervisors. Each enumerator will cover approximately 150-180 houses or 800 individuals. Officials emphasize that enumerators will not ask for personal data, urging households to remain vigilant.

articles.description: Exciting financial gains are just around the corner with pending payments on the verge of resolution. articles.title: Govt ensures uninterrupted fuel supply amid West Asia crisis: Ministry
articles.description: The government has assured uninterrupted supply of LPG, PNG, and CNG despite the West Asia crisis. It urged citizens to avoid panic buying and cut excise duty on petrol and diesel to shield consumers from high global crude prices.
